zh.lifeSummary: William V Aymer DeTaillifer於1220年出生在英國英格蘭。他至少有1個兒子。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: William V Aymer DeTaillifer于1220年出生在英国英格兰。他至少有1个儿子。

nameMeanings.name: Aymer
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: 1 Scottish, English: (i) relationship name from the Middle English personal name Aimar, found in Domesday Book as one of the forms of the Old English name Æðelmǣr ( see Aylmer ). (ii) the Middle English personal name could also be via Old French from a Continental Germanic personal name, either Agimar or (H)adamar.

variants.name: Aylmer
meanings: English: from the Middle English personal name Ailmer, from Old English Æthelmǣr, composed of the elements æthel ‘noble’ and mǣre ‘famous’. This English name has been established in Ireland since the 16th century.

variants.name: Amar
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Jacques, Aime, Alain, Constant, Edouard, Francois, Germaine, Laurent, Prosper, Solange, Stephane, Thierry.  Indian Rajiv, Ajay, Alka, Amar, Arun, Harish, Lata, Meera, Moti, Narain, Praveen, Ramana.  Jewish Yossi, Avi, Avraham, Avram, Chaya, Iian, Itzhak, Mazal, Meryem, Pnina, Shlomo, Shmuel.
French: from a shortened form of the ancient Germanic personal name Ademar, composed of the elements adal ‘noble’ + mar ‘famous’.
Muslim (mainly Maghreb) and Jewish (Sephardic): variant of   Ammar  .

variants.name: Aimers
meanings: Scottish: variant of Aymer with genitival or post-medieval excrescent -s.

variants.name: Hamer
meanings: English (Lancashire): habitational name from a place in Rochdale, Lancashire, named Hamer, from Old English hamor ‘cliff’.
English (of Norman origin): variant of Amer 3 with prosthetic H-.
Dutch: from hamer ‘hammer’, hence a metonymic occupational name for a maker of hammers or a user of a hammer, for example a blacksmith.

variants.name: Amer
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Arabic/Muslim Mohamed, Ahmad, Mohammed, Ahmed, Samir, Alaa, Ali, Amer, Ashraf, Jamal, Maher, Raed.
Muslim: from a variant of ʿAmr, an early Arabic personal name related to Amir 2 and Omar 1.

variants.name: Haymer
meanings: variant of Aymer with prosthetic H-, from the Middle English personal name Aimar, found in Domesday Book as one of the forms of the Old English name Æthelmǣr. Alternatively, the Middle English personal name could also be via Old French from an ancient Germanic personal name, either Agimar or (H)adamar.

variants.name: Domesday Book
meanings: English: locative name from either of two places in Lancs, namely Bispham near Blackpool ( Bischopham, 1086 in Domesday Book ) or Bispham in Croston.

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.
